title: Genesis: Judgment and Mercy
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: judgment and mercy and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: judgment and mercy and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What was Abraham's response when God told him to sacrifice Isaac, and what did God provide instead? Answer: Abraham obeyed and prepared to sacrifice Isaac. God provided a ram caught in a thicket as a substitute.
segments.correct_answer: God provided a ram caught in a thicket as a substitute for Isaac
acceptable_variants: a ram substituted for Isaac on the mountain

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The theological principle where one party bears consequences intended for another; the ram at Moriah anticipates Christ's substitutionary atonement
The mountain where Abraham was commanded to sacrifice Isaac; traditionally identified with the location of Solomon's temple in Jerusalem (2 Chronicles 3:1)
